action.thought_summary: Starting with interaction probing - transportation modes are fundamental to navigation functionality and will reveal workflow expectations.
action.question: What modes of transportation should the navigation directions support (e.g., driving, walking, cycling, public transit)?

action.thought_summary: User redirected to map interaction, search functionality, or visual styling. Currently on turn 2, should probe interaction by turn 3. Will ask about map interaction behaviors like how users select locations and interact with map elements.
action.question: When a user searches for a location and sees it on the map, how should they interact with it — for example, should clicking a map marker show details like address and hours, or should they be able to right-click to set it as a start/end point for navigation?

action.thought_summary: User has been vague on interaction details. Need to ask a more concrete, specific question. Search input behavior is a good target - autocomplete is a common implicit requirement for map search.
action.question: When a user types a location name in the search bar, should the site show autocomplete suggestions as they type, or should it only display results after they submit the search?

action.thought_summary: User has been unsure about broad questions. Need to ask a more concrete, specific question about interaction workflow. Since they mentioned marking locations, I should probe how they envision setting up navigation routes - specifically the input method for start/end points.
action.question: Should users enter their start and end points for navigation using text input fields on the page, or should they be able to click directly on the map to place start and end markers for their route?

action.thought_summary: User has been vague on interaction details. Moving to content/data category per coverage schedule. Asking a concrete question about what location information should be displayed, which is a specific content requirement.
action.question: When a user finds a location through search, what specific details should be displayed about that place — for example, just the name and address, or also information like phone number, website, operating hours, or user ratings?

action.thought_summary: User has been giving vague responses and asking for more specific questions. Turn 6 suggests covering style/UI. I'll ask a very concrete, easy-to-answer question about visual styling preference.
action.question: Should the map display use a light color scheme (like Google Maps default) or a dark color scheme (like many navigation apps use at night)?

action.thought_summary: User has provided some preferences on map interaction (zooming, dragging, marking), search (keyword input), and styling (light pink background, hotpink UI). Key gap: how navigation directions are presented once generated - this is core to the product and hasn't been addressed.
action.question: Once the navigation route is generated, should the directions be displayed as a step-by-step text list alongside the map (like 'Turn left on Main St'), or should the route simply be highlighted on the map with no text directions?

action.thought_summary: The user has been uncertain about many broad questions. I need to ask more concrete, scenario-based questions. I haven't yet explored whether users need to save/bookmark locations, which is a common implicit requirement for map apps. This falls under interaction/workflow category.
action.question: Should users be able to save or bookmark locations they've searched for so they can quickly access them again later, or is this a one-time search-and-navigate tool with no saved history?

action.thought_summary: User has given clear color preferences (light pink background, hotpink UI) but remains uncertain about many functional details. Need to ask more concrete, scenario-based questions to uncover implicit requirements. Current location detection is a common map feature users expect but may not think to specify.
action.question: When a user first opens the website, should the map automatically center on their current location (using GPS/browser location), or should it start at a default location like a city center or the whole country view?

action.thought_summary: User has given vague responses on many topics but confirmed color preferences (light pink background, hotpink UI). Need to probe more specifically about interaction behaviors. Let me ask about a concrete scenario - what happens when search returns multiple matching locations.
action.question: When a user searches for a location and multiple matching results appear (e.g., searching 'Main Street' returns several cities), should the map display numbered markers for all results simultaneously so the user can click one, or should it show a scrollable list panel beside the map where the user selects one result to view on the map?

action.thought_summary: User has given color preferences (light pink background, hotpink UI) and basic interaction expectations (zoom, drag, mark). They've been unsure about many specifics. I should ask a very concrete question about a specific feature. Map view types (satellite, terrain, street) is a common implicit expectation for map applications.
action.question: Should users be able to switch between different map view types, such as a standard street map view and a satellite imagery view, using a toggle button on the map?

action.thought_summary: The user has expressed uncertainty on many topics but did specify light pink background and hotpink UI elements. Middleware suggests focusing on visual style/layout. I'll ask a concrete question about UI layout positioning to uncover implicit preferences about how the interface should be structured.
action.question: Should the search bar and navigation controls be placed in a sidebar panel on the left side of the map, or should they float as an overlay directly on top of the map (like Google Maps' compact search box)?

action.thought_summary: The user has given vague responses on most topics but did specify light pink background and hotpink UI elements. Middleware suggests focusing on visual style/UI. I should ask a concrete, specific question about UI styling that might elicit a clear preference.
action.question: You mentioned hotpink for UI elements — should the search bar and buttons have rounded corners with a soft, modern look, or should they have sharp square corners for a more structured appearance?

I should ask a very specific, concrete question about visual styling that might be easier to answer - perhaps about map markers since those are a key visual element the user hasn't addressed yet.

action.thought_summary: The user has been uncertain about many details but gave clear answers on color preferences (light pink background, hotpink UI elements and markers). The middleware suggests focusing on visual style. I should ask a specific visual question that builds on their known color preference to potentially get a concrete answer.

action.thought_summary: The user has established color preferences (light pink background, hotpink UI elements including markers and route lines) but remains uncertain on many functional and layout details. Middleware suggests focusing on visual style/layout. I'll ask about the overall page layout structure - whether the map fills the entire viewport or shares space with a dedicated panel area.

action.thought_summary: The user has established hotpink UI elements and light pink background but remains uncertain on layout and many specifics. Middleware suggests focusing on visual style/layout/UI. I'll ask a concrete question about the search bar's visual prominence since this touches both layout and style.
action.question: Should the search bar be large and prominently displayed at the top of the page (taking up significant vertical space), or should it be a small compact bar that minimizes visual presence so the map is the dominant element?

action.thought_summary: After 19 turns, the user has been consistently uncertain about most specific details. Key requirements elicited: light pink background, hotpink for all UI elements (markers, route lines, buttons), keyword-based search, map interactions (zoom, drag, mark locations), and navigation with markers. Most other questions received 'not sure' responses. Time to finish.
action.finish_summary: Elicited requirements for the map-based navigation website: 1) Visual Style: Light pink global background color with hotpink for all UI elements including location markers, navigation route lines, search bar, and buttons. 2) Map Interaction: Users can zoom, drag, and mark locations on the map. 3) Search: Keyword-based location search. 4) Navigation: Users can place markers on the map for start/end points to generate routes. Many specific details remain unspecified by the user, including: transportation modes, autocomplete behavior, location detail display format, step-by-step vs. map-only directions, save/bookmark functionality, initial map centering, multiple results display method, map view type switching, layout structure (full-screen map vs. side panel), search bar size/prominence, corner rounding style, and font choice.
